data.title: Enphase Energy signs new safe harbor deal projected to bring nearly $68M in revenue
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (ENPH) on Thursday said it signed a new safe-harbor agreement with a major third-party ownership solar financing provider, its third such deal since July 2025.
  • The agreement, covering IQ9 microinverters manufactured in the U.S., is expected to generate nearly $68 million in revenue over 12 to 24 months starting in 2026.
  • Shipments of the microinverters are scheduled to begin in the first quarter of 2026.
  • ENPH +1.85% premarket to $27.39.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sabre subsidiary launches $1B notes offering
data.description:
• Sabre’s (SABR) wholly-owned subsidiary, Sabre Financial Borrower, has announced an offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of senior secured notes.
  • The precise timing, size, and terms of the offering are subject to market conditions and other factors. Sabre Financial will use the gross proceeds from the sale to fund an intercompany loan to Sabre GLBL.
  • SABR +4.3% premarket to $1.6697
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IonQ gets initiated with Neutral rating at J.P. Morgan on valuation
data.description:
J.P. Morgan began coverage of quantum computing company IonQ (IONQ) with a Neutral rating and a December 2026 price target of $47, citing valuation.

Shares of IonQ rose about 3% premarket on Thursday.

"IonQ is uniquely positioned to lead the next generation of disruptive compute processing technology — quantum computing — which has the potential to unlock multi-trillion dollars in economic value and drive billions in hardware and software revenues in the longer term," said analysts led by Peter Peng.

The analysts noted that while quantum computing remains in the early stages of adoption due to current compute capabilities, IonQ has outlined a clear technology scaling roadmap to drive commercial viability.

In addition, the analysts said that IonQ is developing a comprehensive, full-stack platform — similar to Nvidia’s approach — that integrates hardware (including compute, networking, security, and sensing), software, and ecosystem development.

"This strategy positions IonQ to address all three core areas of quantum technology — quantum computing, quantum sensing, and quantum communications — which in aggregate represents a $46B to $97B TAM [Total Addressable Market] by 2035," said Peng and his team.

The analysts expect IonQ to continue its rapid revenue growth trajectory, supported by its leadership, strategic partnerships (i.e., Hyundai, AstraZeneca, Ansys), commercialization of  products, and robust capital position.

"Despite our favorable view of IonQ’s technology and business outlook, we are initiating coverage at Neutral as we believe the risk/reward is fairly balanced at current price levels, reflecting a steep valuation. Overall, we believe IonQ is positioning itself as a leader in quantum technology with a unique fullstack approach and is well-funded to execute on its ambitious roadmap," said Peng and his team.

data.title: Merck KGaA inks $3B deal to discover Parkinson’s drugs using AI
data.description:
Valo Health on Thursday announced a strategic partnership worth up to $3B with Germany’s Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKKGY) to discover medicines for Parkinson’s disease and related disorders, using the Massachusetts-based biotech’s AI platform.

As part of the deal, Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF) will leverage Valo’s AI-driven human causal biology platform to identify disease targets and use its closed-loop discovery platform to deliver preclinical drugs.

The partnership involves upfront and potential milestone payments worth up to more than $3B in addition to royalties and R&D funding.

“Our research engine is focused on delivering meaningful medicines for patients with high unmet medical needs,” said Amy Kao, Global Head of Merck KGaA's (OTCPK:MKGAF) Neurology & Immunology Research Unit.

Valo Health’s AI‑enabled platforms utilizing human data will help sharpen target selection and stream­line drug discovery, enabling us to advance the most promising candidates faster,” Kao added.

data.title: NetEase declares $0.57/ADS dividend
data.description:
• NetEase (NTES) declares $0.57/ADS quarterly dividend, in-line with prior dividend.
  • Forward yield 1.71%
  • Payable Dec. 19; to shareholders of record Dec. 5; ex-div Dec. 5.
  • See NTES Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Nvidia's results appear positive for server providers such as Dell, HP Enterprise: J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Nvidia's (NVDA) third-quarter results and guidance demonstrated ongoing momentum related to artificial intelligence infrastructure, which appears positive for server solutions companies such as Dell (DELL) and HP Enterprise (HPE), according to J.P. Morgan.

Nvidia reported a 66% year-over-year surge in data center revenue, which proves positive for server original equipment manufacturers, including Dell, HPE and Supermicro (SMCI), J.P. Morgan said. The momentum also appears beneficial for optical component suppliers, including Coherent (COHR), Fabrinet (FN) and Lumentum (LITE).

In fact, every one of the above names was up during pre-market trading on Thursday. Supermicro was up 6%, HPE had increased 1.7% and Dell had ticked up 3%. Meanwhile, Coherent, Fabrinet and Lumentum had climbed 6%, 3% and 7%, respectively.

Nvidia's Networking segment revenue surged 162% year over year.

"Networking revenue more than doubled in F3Q26 from a year ago levels, led by NVLink as well as robust double-digit growth across Spectrum X Ethernet and Quantum X InfiniBand," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Samik Chatterjee, in an investor note. "Importantly, Nvidia highlighted Ethernet wins across Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL) and xAI (X.AI), 'each running OS of choice,' and noted a majority of AI deployments now include switches with Ethernet GPU attach rates roughly on par with InfiniBand."

data.title: AVAX One Technology sets $40M share repurchase program
data.description:
• AVAX One Technology (AVX) has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $40 million of the company's common stock.
  • The board will periodically review the program and may decide to extend its term or increase the authorized amount.
  • AVX +4.67% premarket at $1.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bearish sentiment eases, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Bearish sentiment among individual investors fell to 43.6% for the week ending November 19, down from 49.2% a week earlier, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment was slightly up at 32.6%, compared with 31.6% in the previous week.

Investor sentiment this week was shaped by uneven moves across U.S. markets, volatile sessions, mixed finishes, and sudden jumps in Nasdaq futures tied to Nvidia. Trading was influenced by shifts around the delayed U.S. jobs report, changes in December rate-cut expectations, and a run of tech-focused updates that included valuation worries and sector-specific swings.

European markets climbed on the Nvidia boost and later snapped a four-session losing streak, but slipped on other days when risk-off sentiment returned. Asian markets traded mixed, reflecting updates from China on planning property relief, considering new stimulus for its property sector, maintaining loan prime rates, and broader regional volatility linked to economic data and geopolitical tensions.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also fell during the week, with the figure standing at 19.2%, compared to 23.9% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

data.title: Weekly initial jobless claims dip to 220K
data.description: U.S. initial jobless claims for the week ended Nov. 15 fell by 8K to 220K, according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.
